slides.title: What is a Set?
slides.text: A set is simply a collection of well-defined objects. In Uganda, we often use sets to group things like fruits, numbers, or even countries!

slides.title: Intersection (∩)
slides.text: The Intersection is the region where two sets overlap. It contains items that belong to BOTH groups.

slides.title: Union (∪)
slides.text: The Union combines everything! It includes all elements in Set A, Set B, and the intersection.

slides.title: Difference of Sets
slides.text: If we say 'A only' or (A - B), we want elements that are strictly inside A and NOT in B.
